nodes.label: Clinical pathway
nodes.summary: 16 concept steps in this pathway.

nodes.label: Mechanistic pathway
nodes.summary: 9 concept steps in this pathway.

nodes.label: Emerging topics pathway
nodes.summary: 9 concept steps in this pathway.

nodes.label: Pathogenesis & Immunology
nodes.summary: Mechanisms of demyelination, immune activation, and the cells and cytokines driving MS.

nodes.label: Imaging & Biomarkers
nodes.summary: MRI, optical coherence tomography, and fluid biomarkers that track lesion burden and disease activity.

nodes.label: Clinical Trials & Therapeutics
nodes.summary: Disease-modifying therapies, trial design, and emerging treatment targets.

nodes.label: Clinical Care & Management
nodes.summary: Symptom management, rehabilitation, and the day-to-day care of people with MS.

nodes.label: Epidemiology & Population Health
nodes.summary: Incidence, prevalence, environmental and genetic risk factors, and population-level outcomes.

nodes.label: B Cell–Targeted Therapies
nodes.summary: Anti-CD20 agents (ocrelizumab, ofatumumab, rituximab, ublituximab), mechanism of B-cell depletion, pivotal trial evidence, and safety considerations.

nodes.label: BTK Inhibitors in MS
nodes.summary: Tolebrutinib, fenebrutinib, evobrutinib — CNS-penetrant Bruton's tyrosine kinase inhibitors targeting both B cells and microglia in smoldering MS.

nodes.label: Bladder and Bowel Dysfunction in MS
nodes.summary: Neurogenic bladder and bowel — mechanisms, prevalence, assessment, QoL impact, and management across MS phenotypes.

nodes.label: Blood-Brain Barrier Dysfunction
nodes.summary: How tight-junction disruption and immune cell transmigration drive CNS inflammation — and why BBB integrity is a biomarker of disease activity.

nodes.label: CSF and Blood Biomarkers in MS
nodes.summary: Oligoclonal bands, kappa free light chains, neurofilament light chain (NfL), and GFAP as diagnostic and prognostic biomarkers.

nodes.label: Cognition in MS
nodes.summary: MS-associated cognitive dysfunction — prevalence, affected domains, neuroimaging correlates, assessment tools, and management strategies.

nodes.label: Demyelination and Remyelination
nodes.summary: How inflammatory demyelination destroys myelin sheaths, why remyelination fails, and the path from reversible lesions to permanent axonal damage.

nodes.label: Depression and Anxiety in MS
nodes.summary: Prevalence, biological and reactive mechanisms, bidirectional relationship with disease activity, and evidence-based management of mood disorders in MS.

nodes.label: Disease Course and Disability Progression
nodes.summary: Relapse dynamics, EDSS, long-term disability trajectories, and the transition from relapsing to progressive MS.

nodes.label: Disease-Modifying Therapies — Overview
nodes.summary: The landscape of MS DMTs: mechanism classes, efficacy tiers, key trials, and the shift toward early high-efficacy treatment.

nodes.label: Environmental Risk Factors in MS
nodes.summary: Epstein-Barr virus, vitamin D, smoking, migration patterns, and the evidence that environment shapes 50 % of MS risk.

nodes.label: Equity and Social Determinants of Health in MS
nodes.summary: Structural racism, racial and ethnic disparities in MS diagnosis and access to high-efficacy DMTs, geographic barriers, and the socioeconomic costs of MS.

nodes.label: Exercise and Rehabilitation in MS
nodes.summary: Evidence for exercise as a disease-modifying strategy, physiotherapy, cognitive rehabilitation, and the multidisciplinary model of MS care.

nodes.label: Fatigue in MS
nodes.summary: The most prevalent and disabling MS symptom — mechanisms, validated scales, impact on quality of life, and evidence-based management.

nodes.label: Genetic Susceptibility in MS
nodes.summary: HLA and non-HLA risk loci, GWAS findings, gene-environment interactions, and why MS is not a Mendelian disease.

nodes.label: Immune System Basics for MS
nodes.summary: T cells, B cells, antigen presentation, and the breakdown of self-tolerance that drives MS autoimmunity.

nodes.label: MRI Patterns in MS
nodes.summary: T2/FLAIR lesion characteristics, gadolinium enhancement, central vein sign, paramagnetic rim lesions, and spinal cord imaging in MS diagnosis and monitoring.

nodes.label: MS Epidemiology and Natural History
nodes.summary: Who gets MS, where, and why — incidence, prevalence, geographic gradients, and the longitudinal disease trajectory.

nodes.label: MS Phenotypes and Classification
nodes.summary: RRMS, PPMS, SPMS, CIS, and RIS — definitions, clinical course, and how phenotype guides prognosis and treatment eligibility.

nodes.label: McDonald Criteria
nodes.summary: The international diagnostic criteria for MS — dissemination in space, dissemination in time, and how the 2024 revision incorporates new biomarkers.

nodes.label: PIRA and Smoldering MS
nodes.summary: Progression independent of relapse activity, chronic active lesions, slowly expanding lesions, and the emerging model of smoldering neurodegeneration.

nodes.label: Pediatric-Onset Multiple Sclerosis
nodes.summary: MS diagnosis and management in children and adolescents — IPMSSG criteria, differential from ADEM, treatment considerations, and neurodevelopmental consequences.

nodes.label: Pregnancy and MS
nodes.summary: DMT safety in pregnancy, disease activity changes during gestation, postpartum relapse risk, and family planning counselling.

nodes.label: Sex and Gender Differences in MS
nodes.summary: Why MS preferentially affects women, how sex hormones modulate disease activity, and what gender identity means for MS care.

nodes.label: Shared Decision-Making and Patient Partnership
nodes.summary: How evidence-based shared decision-making in MS differs from paternalistic prescribing — tools, frameworks, and patient priorities.

nodes.label: Treatment Monitoring and Switches
nodes.summary: MRI-based treat-to-target, NfL as a monitoring biomarker, criteria for treatment failure, and when to switch or de-escalate.

nodes.label: What is Multiple Sclerosis?
nodes.summary: Definition and classification of MS as a CNS autoimmune demyelinating disease — and how it differs from other neurological conditions.
